title: Add Meta Tags for Title and Description
description: Update public/index.html to include the specified title and meta description for the landing page.
details:
- Open `public/index.html`.
- Set `<title>YourApp – Welcome</title>`.
- Add `<meta name="description" content="YourApp – Welcome" />` (or use the provided description).
- Ensure these tags are present and correct in the rendered HTML.
test_strategy: Inspect the page source in the browser to confirm the title and meta description are set. Use automated tests (if available) to check for meta tags.

title: Implement Hero CTA Navigation to /signup
description: Configure the Hero section's CTA button to navigate to the /signup route when clicked.
details:
- Use React Router's `useNavigate` hook or `<Link>` component to handle navigation.
- Ensure the CTA button in the Hero section correctly routes to `/signup` on click.
- Confirm accessibility (button is focusable and can be activated via keyboard).
test_strategy: Click the CTA button and verify navigation to `/signup`. Use keyboard navigation to ensure accessibility. Write a test to simulate button click and assert route change.

title: Create LandingPage Component Structure
description: Develop the LandingPage React component with all required sections and import necessary reusable components.
details:
- Create a new file `src/pages/LandingPage.jsx`.
- Structure the component to include: Hero section, Features section (with three Card components), Testimonials section (with Carousel), and Footer.
- Import and use existing `Button`, `Card`, `Carousel`, and `Footer` components from their respective locations.
- Ensure the Hero CTA button is present and set up for navigation (actual navigation logic handled in a later task).
- Use semantic HTML and add appropriate ARIA labels for accessibility.
test_strategy: Render the component in isolation and verify all sections appear without errors. Use React Testing Library to check for presence of each section and imported components. Confirm no console errors during render.

title: Implement Routing for LandingPage
description: Add routing for the LandingPage component at the root path using React Router v6 in App.js.
details:
- In `src/App.js`, import the LandingPage component.
- Add a new Route: `<Route path="/" element={<LandingPage />} />` using React Router v6 syntax.
- Ensure this route renders the LandingPage when navigating to `/`.
- Confirm that other routes (if any) remain unaffected.
test_strategy: Navigate to `/` in the browser and verify the LandingPage renders. Use unit/integration tests to confirm the route matches and renders the correct component.

title: Style LandingPage with CSS Modules and Ensure Responsiveness
description: Create and apply a CSS Module for LandingPage, implementing responsive layouts for mobile, tablet, and desktop breakpoints.
details:
- Create `LandingPage.module.css` alongside the component.
- Apply styles to each section: Hero (with background image), Features (grid for desktop/tablet, stacked for mobile), Testimonials, and Footer.
- Use CSS media queries for breakpoints at 480px, 768px, and 1024px.
- Ensure all components are visually consistent and match the provided Figma mockup.
- Import and use the CSS Module in `LandingPage.jsx`.
test_strategy: Manually test layout at different viewport sizes using browser dev tools. Use visual regression testing (if available) to compare against Figma mockup. Confirm all sections are styled and responsive.
